runner: manual: make -C plugins/plugin-local-inference/native/verify metal-verify metal-verify-multiblock metal-verify-fused (synthetic fixtures, real Metal device); plus the real google/gemma-4-E2B eliza-1 bundle GGUF (head_dim 512, gemma4 arch) generated + benchmarked through the libllama Metal FA path on the same host.

results.metal-verify: PASS 8/8 (turbo3 / turbo4 / turbo3_tcq / qjl / polar + polar+QJL-residual + polar pre-Hadamard ×2), tol=1e-3, max_diff <= 7.629e-06
results.metal-verify-multiblock: PASS 8/8 across 1/2/4/8 blocks-per-workgroup (turbo3 / turbo4 / turbo3_tcq / qjl)
results.metal-verify-fused: PASS 1536/1536 fused-attention outputs across 2 cases (case0 n_heads=4 n_kv_heads=2 n_kv=96 causal; case1 n_heads=8 n_kv_heads=2 n_kv=80 causal), max_diff <= 9.537e-07 — NEW: resolves the prior 'fails by design (no metal_verify cases-array path yet)' item in PLATFORM_MATRIX; the fused QJL-K/TBQ-V flash-attention Metal kernel now verifies. The n_kv_heads=2 GQA/MQA shared-KV geometry is exactly Gemma 4's attention shape.

gemmaCutoverMetal.flashAttnHeadDim512Supported: CONFIRMED in ggml/src/ggml-metal/ggml-metal-device.m FLASH_ATTN_EXT support gate — the supported head-dim set is {112,128,192,256,320,512,576}. Gemma 4 E2B/E4B use head_dim_global=512 with FA as the KV lever (QJL/Polar KV kernels are dimensionally inapplicable per M6), so the cutover's Gemma-4 global-attention path is servable by the Metal FA kernel.
gemmaCutoverMetal.endToEndGeneration: PASS — the ACTUAL elizaos/eliza-1 Gemma-4 base, google/gemma-4-E2B (candidates/gemma-2b-base-v1/text/eliza-1-2b-128k.gguf, Q8_0, 4.61 GiB / 4.65 B params), loaded + generated coherent text on this Metal device with flash_attn auto -> enabled. The loader reported general.architecture=gemma4, attention.key_length=value_length=512 (head_dim 512, the Gemma-4 global-attention dim that drove the head-dim-512 FA support requirement), attention.head_count=8, head_count_kv=1 (MQA / shared-KV), sliding_window=512 (SWA), final_logit_softcapping=30, dual RoPE freq_base 1e6(global)/1e4(swa), context_length=131072. Output: 'The capital of France is Paris. Two famous landmarks there are the Eiffel Tower and the Louvre Museum.' This supersedes the earlier gemma-3-1b-it (head_dim 256) proxy run — the exact gemma-4-E2B head_dim-512 FA path is now exercised directly, not merely inferred from the support gate.
gemmaCutoverMetal.gemma4E2BMetalBench: llama-bench (fork main-b10024, Metal backend MTL,BLAS, -ngl 99 -fa 1) on the real gemma-4-E2B Q8_0: pp512 = 636.44 +/- 125.51 t/s, tg128 = 23.22 +/- 1.67 t/s; libllama vec-FA single-stream load 1141 ms. Cross-backend: the linux-x64-vulkan row reports the same gemma-4-E2B-Q8 at pp512 1486 / tg128 123 t/s on an RTX 5080 (discrete GPU). Both backends generate the model correctly with FA engaged at head_dim 512.

sourceOfTruth: The AGENTS.md §8 Metal kernel gate (metal-verify + -multiblock + -fused) is 40/40 PASS on a real Apple M4 Max Metal device, including the fused-attention cases-array path that was previously authored-pending. The Gemma-4 cutover's Metal requirement — flash-attention at head_dim=512 (the global-attention dim) plus correct end-to-end generation — is now demonstrated DIRECTLY on the real google/gemma-4-E2B eliza-1 base GGUF (not a proxy): correct coherent output + llama-bench throughput through the libllama/llama.cpp Metal FA path, with the loader-reported gemma4 architecture (head_dim 512, MQA, SWA 512, logit-softcap 30) matching the cutover spec.
